extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one wide plateau in the middle that is clearly lower than the values at both the beginning and the end (a long dip).  
R2: Around one side of this wide low region there is a single, narrow spike in the opposite direction (brief peak above the surrounding values).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one wide plateau in the middle that is clearly higher than the values at both the beginning and the end (a long bump).  
R2: Outside this central high plateau there are no strong narrow spikes or dips; the rest of the signal stays roughly flat and low.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one wide region (either high or low) in the middle, similar in length to the plateaus in Classes 0 and 1.  
R2: In addition to that wide region, there is at least one very narrow, sharp dip (a deep, brief valley) inside or near one end of the series, much deeper than the surrounding values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal has one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly lower than the beginning/end baseline), together with one short spike or bump near one side of the plateau.
R2: Outside that low plateau, the rest of the series stays around a roughly constant baseline level without additional long plateaus.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal has one single long high plateau in the middle (values clearly higher than the beginning/end baseline).
R2: Before and after this high plateau the series stays roughly at a constant lower baseline, without any deep dips or extra plateaus.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal has one long plateau (either clearly higher or clearly lower than the baseline), similar in length to class 0/1’s main plateau.
R2: In addition, there is a pronounced, narrow deep dip (values much lower than the surrounding baseline or plateau) located near one end (beginning or end) of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long “valley” segment (values markedly lower than the rest of the series) in the middle region.  
R2: Outside that valley, the series is mostly at a medium level (around the same baseline), possibly with one brief peak or bump, but without any long high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long “plateau” segment (values clearly higher than the rest of the series) in the middle region.  
R2: Before and after this plateau, the series stays roughly at the same lower baseline level, without any deep dips lower than this baseline.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains at least one long “valley” segment (values clearly lower than the rest), similar in length to the class‑0 valley.  
R2: In addition to that long valley, there is at least one extra sharp deep dip (a short, very low trough) near one of the ends, which is clearly lower and shorter than the main valley.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal has one long “low” plateau in the middle (values clearly below the initial level) between an early and a late transition.  
R2: At one end of the low plateau there is a single short “spike” in the opposite direction (a brief high peak when the rest is low, or a brief low dip when the rest is high).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal has one long “high” plateau in the middle (values clearly above the initial and final level) between an early upward transition and a late downward transition.  
R2: Outside this central plateau (at the beginning and end) the signal stays around a constant baseline without any pronounced spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal has one long plateau (either high or low) similar to classes 0 and 1, but also contains a second, shorter plateau of opposite level near one end.  
R2: This second plateau is reached via a sharp spike or dip that goes clearly beyond the baseline/plateau levels, so the series shows two distinct transitions separated by a brief extreme excursion.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly below the surrounding baseline, roughly between indices 40 and 100).  
R2: The series has one pronounced spike on one side of that low plateau (either before or after), where the value is clearly higher than the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau in the middle (values clearly above the surrounding baseline, roughly between indices 40 and 100).  
R2: Both sides before and after this plateau stay close to a common baseline level without any very deep dips or sharp spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long region where values differ strongly from the baseline (either a long low plateau or a long high plateau).  
R2: In addition to that long region, there is at least one very sharp, narrow deep dip on one side (value much lower than both the baseline and the plateau, over a short interval).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly lower than both the early and late parts).  
R2: There is no long low plateau at the end of the series (final segment returns to the higher baseline level).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series contains one long high plateau in the middle (values clearly higher than both the early and late parts).  
R2: There is no long high plateau at the end of the series (final segment returns to the lower baseline level).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau (either high or low) in the middle that clearly differs from the starting baseline.  
R2: The series also contains a pronounced, brief dip or spike near the end that sharply deviates from the surrounding values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly lower than both the early and late parts of the series for many consecutive time steps).
R2: The series does not contain any long high plateau in the middle; any high excursion is short (a brief spike) compared to the long low plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series contains one long high plateau in the middle (values clearly higher than both the early and late parts of the series for many consecutive time steps).
R2: Outside this central high plateau, the values stay roughly at a similar baseline level before and after, without any long low plateau or deep sharp dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains at least one long low plateau or a very deep, narrow dip near the end, whose values are markedly below the baseline.
R2: The series also contains at least one high or baseline segment that is clearly higher than this low region, so that the pattern shows a strong “drop then return” near or after the middle of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long low plateau (values clearly below the baseline, around 0.1–0.2) in the middle segment, between two higher-baseline regions (around 0.45–0.55).  
R2: There is at most one brief excursion/spike above the high baseline, and if present it appears near the end of the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau (values clearly above baseline, around 0.65–0.85) in the middle segment, with lower, roughly constant baseline regions before and after.  
R2: Outside this single high plateau, the signal stays close to a constant baseline (no pronounced dips below or spikes above that baseline).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau on the opposite side of the baseline from the surrounding regions (either a long low plateau between two high-baseline parts, or a long high plateau between two low-baseline parts).  
R2: In addition to this long plateau, there is at least one sharp and deep, short-duration dip (or spike) near one end of the series that is much more extreme than the long plateau and the baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly below the baseline for many consecutive points).  
R2: Outside that low plateau, values stay around a roughly constant baseline, possibly with a single sharp positive spike near one end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau in the middle (values clearly above the baseline for many consecutive points).  
R2: Outside that high plateau, values stay around a roughly constant baseline with no large additional spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau (either high or low) similar in width to classes 0 or 1.  
R2: In addition to that plateau, there is at least one extra sharp, narrow dip or spike near an end of the series that breaks away strongly from the surrounding baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly lower than both the early and late segments for many consecutive time steps).
R2: Outside this low plateau, the baseline stays roughly constant and there is at most one brief spike above that baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau in the middle (values clearly higher than both the early and late segments for many consecutive time steps).
R2: Outside this high plateau, the baseline stays roughly constant and there are no additional deep dips or sharp spikes beyond normal noise.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains both a long plateau (either high or low) and, separated from it in time, at least one very sharp, narrow dip (value suddenly much lower than its immediate neighbors).
R2: The baseline before the first extreme event and after the last one returns to approximately the same level.
